findings_details.title: makeFakeTerminalManager duplicated from app-routes-auto-update.test.ts
findings_details.body: The function is defined identically in both test files (lines 24-60 in the original, lines 25-55 in the new file). The new copy also has a subtly different destroy() — it omits the emitter.emit('exit') call present in the original. Extract makeFakeTerminalManager into a shared test helper, e.g. server/__tests__/helpers/fake-terminal-manager.ts, and import it in both test files.

findings_details.title: Regression tests verify only that non-gated routes pass; never verify that auto-update routes still require auth
findings_details.body: The two scope-regression tests confirm that /api/health is not blocked. They do not verify that /api/admin/auto-update/* still returns 403 when the bearer token is missing or wrong. A refactoring that accidentally removed router.use('/api/admin/auto-update', requireBearerToken(...)) entirely would pass both regression tests, because they only probe a non-admin endpoint. Add a test: when TOKEN_ENV is set but the bearer header is absent, a POST to /api/admin/auto-update/snapshot-and-drain must return 403, confirming the gate was not removed by the scope fix.

findings_details.title: router.use path-scoped middleware strips prefix from req.path, causing truncated paths in auth-rejection log lines
findings_details.body: Express router.use('/api/admin/auto-update', fn) strips the matched prefix before passing the request to fn, so inside requireBearerToken, req.path is '/snapshot-and-drain' rather than '/api/admin/auto-update/snapshot-and-drain'. The auth middleware logs req.path on rejection (admin-auth.ts lines 34, 42, 51, 59), so operators will see bare suffixes in rejection logs rather than the full path. The fix is to use router.use('/api/admin/auto-update/:rest(*)', ...) or, more simply, register the auth middleware directly on each route handler rather than as a prefix-scoped use(). Alternatively, document the truncation in a comment so it is not mistaken for a bug later.

findings_details.title: Replay fallback test relies on readSnapshot's missing runtime validation
findings_details.body: The test writes a snapshot JSON without a 'sessions' field and expects a 500. This works because readSnapshot casts the partial object as AutoUpdateSnapshot (type assertion, no runtime check) and replayAll then throws TypeError when iterating undefined. If readSnapshot is later hardened to validate that sessions is an array, it returns null → handleReplay returns 404 not 500 → this test breaks without any regression in the production code. The test is coupling to an internal fragility rather than a defined contract.

findings_details.title: No test asserts that gated endpoints still reject unauthenticated requests after path scoping
findings_details.body: The two regression tests verify that /api/health is NOT blocked by the bearer middleware. But there is no test in this file verifying that the three auto-update endpoints ARE still blocked when the token is absent or wrong. The path-scoped middleware could silently be changed to a non-matching prefix (e.g. a typo) and the regression suite would still pass green. The fix to issue #202 needs a positive guard: at least one test that POSTs to /api/admin/auto-update/snapshot-and-drain without a bearer token and expects 403, confirming the gate is still in effect.

findings_details.title: homeDir falls back to empty string when HOME is unset, creating relative snapshot paths
findings_details.body: index.ts passes homeDir: process.env.HOME ?? '' to createAutoUpdateRoutes. If HOME is unset (container images, restricted environments), homeDir is ''. snapshotPath('') returns '.c3/auto-update-snapshot.json' (relative), and writeSnapshot calls mkdirSync(join('', '.c3'), ...) which creates .c3/ relative to the process cwd. This silently changes where snapshots are written and read, making auto-update non-functional in a way that produces no startup error. Fix: fail fast at startup if HOME is unset, or document the constraint in config.ts and validate there.
